sow.objective: Design and deploy a scalable, secure, and GDPR-compliant e-commerce platform on AWS supporting 10,000 concurrent users, 1 million product catalog, real-time inventory synchronization, and 99.95% uptime SLA.
success_criteria: Architecture approved by security team before week 2
99.95% uptime achieved in production (≤22 minutes downtime/month)
Sub-200ms p99 latency at 10K concurrent users verified via load testing
GDPR compliance verified (data residency, audit logging, DPA)
SOC 2 Type II controls documented and operational
Incident response runbooks completed before go-live

deliverables.name: Architecture Design Document
deliverables.description: Comprehensive technical architecture specification including service selection rationale, network design, security posture, compliance mapping, cost analysis, and implementation roadmap
acceptance_criteria: Document covers all 9 AWS service components
Security & compliance sections address GDPR and SOC 2

deliverables.name: Infrastructure as Code (Terraform Modules)
deliverables.description: Production-ready Terraform modules for VPC, EC2, RDS, ElastiCache, OpenSearch, ALB, KMS, CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and deployment automation scripts
acceptance_criteria: All modules tested in staging environment
State management configured (S3 backend with locking)

deliverables.name: Application API & Core Services
deliverables.description: RESTful API implementation including user authentication, product catalog search, shopping cart, order management, inventory sync service, and payment processing integration
acceptance_criteria: Integration tests passing for all critical paths
Performance benchmarks validated (P50, P95, P99 latencies)

deliverables.name: Security & Compliance Validation
deliverables.description: OWASP Top 10 security testing, penetration testing results, GDPR compliance checklist, SOC 2 Type II control documentation, and security hardening recommendations
acceptance_criteria: No Critical or High severity vulnerabilities found
GDPR compliance verified (data residency, encryption, audit logs)

deliverables.name: Load & Performance Testing Report
deliverables.description: Load test results at 10K, 15K, and 20K concurrent users; failover testing; database performance analysis; cache effectiveness; recommendations for optimization
acceptance_criteria: Cache hit ratio >85% for product queries

deliverables.name: Operational Runbooks & Incident Response Playbook
deliverables.description: Comprehensive documentation covering deployment procedures, rollback procedures, on-call playbooks, common issues and troubleshooting, disaster recovery procedures, and team training materials
acceptance_criteria: All team members trained and signed off
RTO/RPO defined for each critical service

deliverables.name: Cost Model & Optimization Report
deliverables.description: Detailed monthly cost breakdown by service, cost optimization recommendations (Reserved Instances, Spot pricing, consolidation), and growth scenarios (2x, 5x scale)
acceptance_criteria: Growth scenarios showing cost at 2x and 5x scale
Reserved Instance recommendations with payback period

deliverables.name: Go-Live Checklist & Cutover Plan
deliverables.description: DNS migration plan, traffic switch procedure, health check validation, rollback procedure, and post-go-live support schedule

phases.name: Phase 1: Design & Planning
phases.description: Architecture review, security assessment, compliance validation, cost approval, and team preparation
tasks: Security risk assessment and threat modeling
Team skill assessment and training plan
success_criteria: Security and compliance risks identified and mitigated

phases.name: Phase 2: Infrastructure Deployment
phases.description: VPC, networking, security groups, IAM, databases, caching, search, monitoring, and backup infrastructure setup via Terraform
tasks: VPC, subnets, NAT gateways, route tables
IAM roles, policies, and assume role relationships
ALB with target groups and health checks
RDS Aurora multi-AZ setup with read replica
OpenSearch domain with appropriate shard allocation
S3 buckets with encryption and versioning
CloudTrail, CloudWatch Logs, VPC Flow Logs
success_criteria: Security groups and IAM policies validated

phases.name: Phase 3: Application Development
phases.description: API development, business logic, payment integration, admin dashboard, and integration with AWS services
tasks: User authentication and session management (JWT + Redis)
Product catalog API with pagination and full-text search
Stripe payment integration with webhook handlers
success_criteria: All core APIs implemented and tested

phases.name: Phase 4: Testing & Hardening
phases.description: Load testing, security testing, failover testing, performance tuning, documentation, and go-live preparation
tasks: Team training and on-call rotation setup
success_criteria: Team trained and ready for production support

team_cost.note: Team cost amortized; actual may be lower post-launch

assumptions: Application code written in Node.js or Python (stateless, 12-factor compliant)
Peak load: 10K concurrent users = ~50 req/sec baseline
Product catalog: 1M products with minimal daily updates
Data residency: EU-WEST-1 (Ireland) only; no multi-region until post-launch
Team: 4 full-time engineers with basic AWS knowledge
No legacy systems to migrate; greenfield development
Budget includes infrastructure and team costs only (no hardware, software licenses)
constraints: Timeline: 10 weeks from architecture approval to production
Compliance: GDPR + SOC 2 Type II required
Payment: Stripe API (no custom payment processor)

risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Weekly progress reviews; parallel Phase 2 + Phase 3 work starting week 3; pre-staging of non-blocking tasks
Code review process for Terraform; infrastructure testing in staging; automated validation with terraform plan
Load testing in week 9 at 15K users; database query optimization; caching strategy validation
GDPR consultant review (optional); CloudTrail validation; data residency verification before go-live
Documentation throughout project; knowledge sharing sessions; cross-training on critical components

acceptance_criteria: Architecture approved by security and compliance teams
Sub-200ms p99 latency at 10K concurrent users
GDPR compliance verified (data residency, encryption, audit logging)
SOC 2 Type II controls documented and operational
All acceptance criteria in 8 deliverables signed off
Team trained on incident response and on-call procedures

sign_off.notes: SOW requires stakeholder approval before Phase 1 commences. Any scope changes after Phase 1 sign-off will be documented in a change order.
